signals.question: US × Iran ceasefire by April 15?
evidence: Iran explicitly rejected US 15-point ceasefire plan March 25, calling it 'maximalist, unreasonable' — Al Jazeera https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2026/3/25/iran-calls-us-proposal-to-end-war-maximalist-unreasonable

Iran FM: 'no peace talks with US have taken place' and Iran has 'no plans for negotiations' — NBC News https://www.nbcnews.com/world/middle-east/live-blog/live-updates-iran-war-talks-trump-peace-plan-troops-hormuz-rcna265051
Pakistan confirms backchannel talks ongoing but Iran's public rejection stands — Jerusalem Post https://www.jpost.com/middle-east/article-891422
VP Vance reportedly chided Netanyahu for overselling regime change chances — Times of Israel https://www.timesofisrael.com/liveblog-march-27-2026/

Israel struck IRGC navy commander + Isfahan industrial targets March 26-27; Iran fired missiles at central Israel — escalation continuing — Al Jazeera https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2026/3/26/us-israel-war-on-iran-whats-happening-on-day-27-of-attacks
Resolution requires 'clear public confirmation from both governments' — extremely high bar when no direct talks exist and only indirect backchannel via Pakistan
signals.reasoning: Iran has publicly rejected the US ceasefire plan, denied any negotiations, and hardened its 5-point counterdemands (including reparations and Hormuz sovereignty). With only indirect backchannel via Pakistan, active military escalation on both sides, and 19 days until close, 28.5% dramatically overestimates the probability of a formal ceasefire requiring public confirmation from both governments.

signals.question: US × Iran ceasefire by April 30?
evidence: Iran rejected US 15-point ceasefire plan March 25; Iran's 5 counterdemands include reparations and Strait of Hormuz sovereignty — NPR https://www.npr.org/2026/03/25/nx-s1-5760675/iran-war-military-deployment
Pakistan backchannel confirmed but Iran publicly refuses to negotiate — Jerusalem Post, Washington Post https://www.washingtonpost.com/world/2026/03/27/pakistan-us-iran-war-emerging-peace-mediator/
Military escalation continuing: Israel struck IRGC navy commander + Isfahan steel factories; Iran fired missiles at central Israel — Al Jazeera
Trump extended energy strike pause to April 6 but this has not produced Iranian engagement — NPR https://www.npr.org/2026/03/26/nx-s1-5761882/iran-war-peace-conditions
Vance reportedly chided Netanyahu for overselling regime change — US administration not unified on war aims — Times of Israel
Historical base rate: wars of this scale (US/Israel vs regional power) rarely produce formal ceasefires within 2 months of initiation, especially with fundamental disagreements on terms
signals.reasoning: The fundamental diplomatic impasse — Iran refusing direct talks, demanding reparations and Hormuz sovereignty, while US-Israel escalate militarily — makes a formal ceasefire requiring public confirmation from both sides within 34 days highly unlikely. The Pakistan backchannel is promising but Iran's public stance remains intransigent. At 40.5%, the market significantly overprices YES.

signals.question: US × Iran ceasefire by April 7?
evidence: Iran rejected US ceasefire plan 2 days ago; FM denies any talks are occurring — Al Jazeera, NBC News
Trump's energy strike pause deadline expires April 6 — if anything, escalation resumes before this market closes — NPR https://www.npr.org/2026/03/26/nx-s1-5761882/iran-war-peace-conditions
Only 11 days until close; no direct talks, no framework for agreement; resolution requires mutual public confirmation from both governments
Pakistan backchannel ongoing but insufficient to produce formal agreement in 11 days when Iran publicly refuses to negotiate
signals.reasoning: With Iran publicly refusing talks, active military escalation on both sides, and only 11 days remaining, a formal ceasefire requiring public confirmation from both governments by April 7 is near-impossible. Trump's energy pause deadline expires April 6 — if anything, escalation resumes before this market closes.
